Dockerfile.general_idea: Switch the base image from a Debian-based Maven/JDK 11 image to an Alpine-based Liberica JDK 21 image to align with the Java 21 migration.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The application requires Java 21 runtime features and compatibility; running on JDK 11 would cause runtime failures or missing API support for Spring Boot 3.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Replace Debian package manager (apt-get) with Alpine package manager (apk) for installing system dependencies.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The new base image is Alpine Linux, which does not support apt-get; using apt-get would result in command not found errors during build.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Update Node.js installation method from NodeSource setup script to NVM to support a newer Node.js version compatible with the new environment.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The previous NodeSource setup for Node 12 is incompatible with the new Alpine environment and outdated; NVM provides a reliable way to install Node 16 on Alpine.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Install Maven explicitly via apk since the base image no longer includes it.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The new base image is a minimal JDK image without Maven; the build step requires Maven to compile the project.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Update the entrypoint to run the compiled JAR file instead of starting a bash shell.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The previous CMD started a shell, which is not suitable for running the application in production; the new entrypoint executes the Spring Boot application.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Add build step to compile the Java application using Maven within the Docker build process.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The application needs to be compiled into a JAR file before it can be run; the previous image likely assumed pre-built artifacts or a different workflow.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Copy the entire application source code into the Docker image to enable the build step.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The Maven build step requires access to the source code and pom.xml; without copying the files, the build would fail.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Set environment variables for Node.js version and NVM directory to ensure consistent Node.js usage.
Dockerfile.why_exists: NVM requires specific environment variables to function correctly; without them, the Node.js installation would not be accessible.

Dockerfile.general_idea: Install bash and libstdc++ as dependencies required by the build tools and runtime.
Dockerfile.why_exists: Some build scripts or native libraries may require bash and libstdc++; missing these would cause build or runtime failures.
Dockerfile.after:
RUN apk add bash
RUN apk add --no-cache libstdc++

Dockerfile.general_idea: Install git to support build processes that may require version control information.
Dockerfile.why_exists: The build process or application configuration may rely on git metadata; without git, these operations would fail.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.4 to align with Java 21 and Spring Boot 3 ecosystem.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x is incompatible with Java 21 features and Spring Framework 6; migration to Boot 3 is required for JDK 21 support.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Compiler and runtime must target Java 21 to utilize new language features and ensure compatibility with Spring Boot 3.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade spring-cloud-gateway-mvc to 4.1.3 for Spring Boot 3 compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: Version 3.0.1 depends on Spring Framework 5, which is incompatible with Spring Boot 3 (Spring Framework 6).

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated springfox dependencies with springdoc-openapi for API documentation.
pom.xml.why_exists: Springfox is incompatible with Spring Boot 3/Spring Framework 6 due to package refactoring (jakarta.* vs javax.*); springdoc is the supported alternative.
